items.label: storefront-dev
messages: Files already exist! Let me check their current state and see if tests pass:
Good, the component files exist. Let me check the test files and run the tests:
Now let me run the tests to see their current state:
All 26 tests pass. Now let me validate the dev server works:

Dev server returns HTTP 200. Now let me check the ErrorBoundary requirement from the spec. Looking at the QuickViewModal, I need to verify it wraps ProductView in an ErrorBoundary per the system instructions:
The system instructions mandate wrapping ProductView in an ErrorBoundary when rendered inside a custom container (modal). Let me add that:
Now let me re-run the tests to confirm they still pass:

All 26 tests pass. The uncovered lines 35,39 are the ErrorBoundary's `getDerivedStateFromError` and the error render — that's expected since we'd need to intentionally throw in a test to cover them. Let me check the validate-app.sh hook:
The Quick View feature doesn't add new routes/pages — it's an overlay on existing PLP pages — so no validation hook changes needed.  Now let me do the SSR safety checklist:
Good — no SSR violations. Now let me commit and complete the pipeline:

The **storefront-dev** phase for **product-quick-view** is complete. Here's a summary of what was delivered:  ## Deliverables  ### Files Modified/Created | Action | Path | Description | |---|---|---| | **EXISTS** | `overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.jsx` | ProductTile override with Quick View overlay bar | | **EXISTS** | `overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.test.js` | 12 unit tests for tile overlay | | **EXISTS** | `overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.jsx` | QuickViewModal with ErrorBoundary | | **EXISTS** | `overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.test.js` | 14 unit tests for modal |  ### Key Implementation Details 1. **ProductTile Override** — Wraps base `ProductTile` with `role="group"` container; adds full-width semi-transparent overlay bar at bottom of image area with slide-up animation on desktop hover, always visible on mobile 2. **QuickViewModal** — Uses `useProductViewModal` hook to fetch full product data; renders `ProductView` with `showFullLink={true}` and `imageSize="sm"`; includes loading spinner, error state for unavailable products, and `ErrorBoundary` wrapping `ProductView` to prevent modal crashes from destroying the page 3. **Accessibility** — `aria-label` on both overlay bar and modal; keyboard focusable bar with `_focus` reveal; Escape closes modal; focus trapped inside modal 4. **Guards** — Hidden for product sets/bundles; guarded against missing `productId`; `e.preventDefault()` + `e.stopPropagation()` to prevent PDP navigation  ### Verification - ✅ **26/26 unit tests pass**  - ✅ **Dev server returns HTTP 200** (webpack resolves all modules) - ✅ **SSR safety** — No `window`/`document` access, no `Date.now()`/`Math.random()` - ✅ **All `data-testid` attributes** present per E2E contract (`quick-view-btn`, `quick-view-modal`, `quick-view-spinner`, `quick-view-error`, `quick-view-render-error`)

cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && npm run pipeline:complete product-quick-view storefront-dev 2>&1

items.label: storefront-unit-test
messages: Now let me explore the actual implementation files and existing test patterns:
Good - the test files already exist (placeholder stubs perhaps). Let me read all the source and test files:
Good - the implementation and test files already exist with comprehensive coverage. Let me run the tests to check if they pass:
All 26 tests pass across both test suites. Let me check what's uncovered and see if I should add tests for the error boundary (lines 35, 39 in quick-view-modal):
The uncovered lines are the `ProductViewErrorBoundary` error path (lines 35, 39) and line 28 in product-tile. Let me add tests for the error boundary to improve coverage:
Line 28 branch coverage is about `productName` fallback. Let me add tests for the error boundary (to cover lines 35, 39) and for the productName fallback. I'll add them to the existing test files:
Now let me also add a test for product-tile's `productName` fallback (line 28 branch - when product has `name` but not `productName`):
Now let me run all tests again to verify everything passes:
All 30 tests pass with **100% code coverage** across all metrics (statements, branches, functions, lines) for both components. Now let me commit and complete the pipeline:

The **storefront-unit-test** phase for **product-quick-view** is complete. Here's a summary:  ## Results  ### Tests Written & Passing: 30/30 ✅  **QuickViewModal tests** (`quick-view-modal/index.test.js`) — 15 tests: - Loading spinner when fetching - ProductView rendered when loaded - Correct `data-testid` on modal - Accessible `aria-label` with product name - Modal hidden when `isOpen=false` - Close button triggers `onClose` - Error state for unavailable products - Product data passed to ProductView (name, price) - "View Full Details" link rendered - Add to Cart button rendered - `showFullLink={true}` prop forwarded - `isProductLoading` forwarding - Escape key closes modal - Aria-label fallback for missing product name - **Error boundary catches render errors** (new — covers `ProductViewErrorBoundary`)  **ProductTile override tests** (`product-tile/index.test.js`) — 15 tests: - Quick View bar renders on standard products - Eye icon + "Quick View" text present - Correct aria-label with product name - Bar hidden for product sets - Bar hidden for product bundles - Bar hidden when productId missing - All props forwarded to base ProductTile - Clicking bar opens modal - `preventDefault` called on click - `stopPropagation` called on click - Modal hides on close - Container has `role="group"` - **Aria-label uses `name` fallback when `productName` missing** (new) - **Aria-label empty suffix when no name fields** (new) - **Base tile renders even without Quick View bar** (new)  ### Coverage: 100% across all metrics | File | Stmts | Branch | Funcs | Lines | |---|---|---|---|---| | product-tile/index.jsx | 100% | 100% | 100% | 100% | | quick-view-modal/index.jsx | 100% | 100% | 100% | 100% |

cat >> /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront/overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.test.js << 'TESTEOF' ↵ // --- Additional edge cases --- ↵ test('overlay bar aria-label uses name when p
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh all "test(storefront): add unit tests for product-quick-view components with 100% coverage" 2>&1
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && npm run pipeline:complete product-quick-view storefront-unit-test 2>&1

items.label: e2e-author
messages: Now I have a complete picture. Let me create the E2E test file.  **Intent: Creating E2E test file**  Key `data-testid` contract map: - `quick-view-btn` — overlay bar trigger on product tiles - `quick-view-modal` — modal content container - `quick-view-spinner` — loading spinner in modal - `quick-view-error` — error/unavailable state in modal - `quick-view-render-error` — render error boundary fallback
Now let me run the self-review gate to check for banned patterns:
No banned patterns found. Let me verify the file is syntactically valid and then commit:

The only real error in our file is line 166 — the redundant type guard `winner === 'crash'` after the crash check. The rest are `node_modules` type compatibility issues unrelated to us. Let me fix that:
Now let me verify the file looks correct and commit:
14 tests across 2 describe blocks (12 happy-path + 2 edge cases). Now commit and complete:

**E2E test authoring complete.** Here's a summary of what was delivered:  ## `e2e/product-quick-view.spec.ts` — 14 Playwright E2E Tests  ### Test Coverage  | # | Test | What it verifies | |---|---|---| | 1 | Quick View button appears on PLP | `data-testid="quick-view-btn"` rendered, has `aria-label` | | 2 | Button contains "Quick View" text | Text content of the overlay bar | | 3 | Clicking opens modal with data | Modal opens, URL unchanged, `aria-label` matches pattern | | 4 | Modal shows spinner → content | Loading state transitions to ProductView with Add to Cart / View Full Details | | 5 | Modal close via X button | Chakra `ModalCloseButton` hides modal | | 6 | Modal close via Escape key | Keyboard dismiss works | | 7 | No PDP navigation | URL unchanged after open + close cycle | | 8 | View Full Details link to PDP | Link visible inside modal, `href` matches `/product/` pattern | | 9 | Button accessibility (semantic `<button>`) | Tag is `button`, has `aria-label` | | 10 | Modal aria-label with product name | Pattern: "Quick view for {name}" | | 11 | Multiple buttons for multiple products | Count ≥ 1, each has unique label | | 12 | Different products show different modal data | Open/close 2 Quick Views, compare aria-labels | | 13 | Sets/bundles edge case | All rendered buttons have valid `aria-label` | | 14 | PLP resilient after modal close | Product tiles survive open/close cycle |  ### Patterns Used - **Three-outcome assertion** (content / error / crash) for modal opening - **Crash page detection** with stack trace capture - **Browser diagnostics** (console errors + failed requests) captured per test - **`domcontentloaded`** for navigation, explicit locator waits for readiness - **Zero banned patterns** (`networkidle`, `waitForTimeout`) — verified via grep gate

The **create-draft-pr** phase is complete.
